root_motivation: The MQO bi-toolkit chain (recommender → emitter → bundle) needs a deterministic last hop that converts a typed chart-recommendation.v1 + rows into a renderer-ready Vega-Lite v5 JSON spec, so an LLM orchestrating 'chart this' never hand-assembles VL5 JSON and guesses at schema URLs, type keywords, or aggregation rules.
user_persona: An LLM orchestrator (or human developer) running inside the mqo-mcp-server bi-toolkit pipeline; calls emit(recommendation, rows) and hands the returned spec to any VL5 renderer or downstream bundle step.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Line recommendation (x=year temporal, y=revenue quantitative) over matching rows emits a spec with $schema pointing at VL5, mark:'line', encoding.x.field=='year' with type temporal, and encoding.y.field=='revenue' with type quantitative.
data.values equals the input rows verbatim (inline embedding, no mutation or reordering of row objects).
A Bar recommendation emits mark:'bar' with the nominal field on the x channel.
An encoding channel referencing a field absent from every row returns EmitError (does not emit a broken spec that renders blank).
A BigNumber recommendation emits a 'text'-mark spec with the measure field in encoding.text.
A quantitative y-channel gets aggregate:'sum' by default, but a semi_additive measure does NOT get aggregate:'sum'.
The emitted spec is deterministic and round-trips through serde_json without key-reordering surprises (stable object key order via a fixed struct).

scope: Pure Rust library: emit(recommendation, rows) -> Result<serde_json::Value, EmitError>
Map chart-recommendation.v1 mark + encoding channels to Vega-Lite v5 JSON spec
Apply aggregate:sum for quantitative channels (unless semi_additive)
Refuse to emit if any encoding channel references a field absent from every row
Handle BigNumber mark as text-mark with encoding.text
Handle Table mark as text-mark + _render:table extension key
non_goals: No rendering: no PNG, SVG, browser, or vl-convert dependency
Does not call the mqo-chart-recommender crate — reads chart-recommendation.v1 as raw JSON
No network access of any kind
No live render feature (--render is an explicit out-of-scope future feature flag)
Does not validate the full VL5 spec against the JSON Schema (structural assembly only)

additional.semi_additive_guard: A semi_additive quantitative channel must never receive aggregate:sum

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a Vega-Lite spec emitter?
five_whys_trace.a: The MQO pipeline has chart-recommendation.v1 + rows but no step that turns them into a renderer-ready spec.

five_whys_trace.q: Why can't the LLM just assemble the VL5 JSON itself?
five_whys_trace.a: LLMs guess at the $schema URL, data_type keyword spelling, and whether to add aggregate, producing specs that render blank or lie; a deterministic function eliminates all three failure modes.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does a blank-rendering spec matter?
five_whys_trace.a: The LLM trusts the spec it emitted; a blank chart looks like no data, not a bug, so the error is invisible and silently propagates downstream to the bi-asset-bundle.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the emitter depend on the mqo-chart-recommender Rust crate?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. Reads chart-recommendation.v1 as serde_json::Value. This keeps the dep tree minimal and avoids a circular path dep.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What aggregate should a Table mark get?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Table emits mark:'text' + _render:'table' extension key. It does not need aggregate logic — the consumer renders the raw rows as a grid.
